label: AWS CloudWatch
description: Query AWS CloudWatch metrics, logs, and alarms using your AWS Access Key ID and Secret Access Key.

form.title: Connect AWS CloudWatch
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read metrics, logs, and alarms from AWS CloudWatch.

methods.label: Access Key ID + Secret Access Key
methods.description: Paste an IAM user's Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. Quickest path — no OAuth setup required. Recommended for most users.
methods.how_to:
## Create an IAM user with CloudWatch read access

1. Sign in to the [AWS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/) and open **IAM**.
2. Go to **Users → Create user**. Give it a name like `anton-cloudwatch`.
3. On the **Set permissions** step, choose **Attach policies directly** and attach `CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess` (or `CloudWatchFullAccess` if you also want Anton to manage alarms).
4. Complete the wizard. Open the new user, go to the **Security credentials** tab.
5. Under **Access keys**, click **Create access key** → choose **Application running outside AWS** → click through to **Create access key**.
6. Copy the **Access key ID** and **Secret access key** — the secret is shown only once. Store it securely.
7. Paste both values and your target AWS region into the fields below.

## Notes

- Use the **principle of least privilege**: `CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess` covers metrics, logs, and alarms reads without write permissions.
- If your account uses SCPs or permission boundaries, make sure `cloudwatch:*` and `logs:*` reads are not blocked.
- To rotate keys, generate a new pair in IAM, update Anton, then deactivate the old key.

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID
fields.description: The 20-character Access Key ID from your IAM user's Security credentials tab.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key
fields.description: The 40-character secret paired with the Access Key ID. Shown only once at creation time.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region where your CloudWatch data lives. You can query cross-region later via separate connectors.

fields.name: log_group_name
fields.label: Default Log Group (optional)
fields.description: Optional. Pre-fill a CloudWatch Logs log group name Anton should query by default. You can override this per query.

methods.label: IAM Role (assume-role via keys)
methods.description: Provide a base IAM Access Key pair plus a Role ARN. Anton will call STS AssumeRole before each CloudWatch request — useful when your security policy enforces role-based access rather than long-lived user keys.
methods.how_to:
## Set up IAM Role assumption

Use this method when your organization's security policy requires all access to go through assumed roles rather than long-lived IAM user credentials.

1. Create (or identify) an **IAM user** that has `sts:AssumeRole` permission. Generate an Access Key pair for it (see the Access Key method above for steps).
2. Create (or identify) the **IAM role** Anton should assume. In the role's **Trust policy**, add the IAM user ARN as a trusted principal:
   ```json
   { "Effect": "Allow", "Principal": { "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/anton-cloudwatch" }, "Action": "sts:AssumeRole" }
   ```
3. Attach `CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess` (or a custom policy) to the role's **Permissions**.
4. Copy the role's **ARN** from the role's Summary page (format: `arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/ROLE-NAME`).
5. Paste the user's Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, the Role ARN, and your region below.

## Notes

- Anton will call `sts:AssumeRole` and cache temporary credentials for up to 1 hour before refreshing.
- If the role has an **External ID** configured on the trust policy, fill that field too.

fields.name: aws_access_key_id
fields.label: Access Key ID (caller identity)
fields.description: The Access Key ID of the IAM user that has permission to call sts:AssumeRole.

fields.name: aws_secret_access_key
fields.label: Secret Access Key (caller identity)
fields.description: Paired with the Access Key ID above.

fields.name: role_arn
fields.label: Role ARN
fields.description: The full ARN of the IAM role Anton will assume before querying CloudWatch.

fields.name: external_id
fields.label: External ID (optional)
fields.description: Only needed if the role's trust policy enforces an ExternalId condition.

fields.name: aws_region
fields.label: AWS Region
fields.description: The AWS region where your CloudWatch data lives.
